foodsharingIdHelp.title: Find your Foodsharing ID
foodsharingIdHelp.intro: Use this number in any field that asks for your Foodsharing ID.
foodsharingIdHelp.step3: Go to your profile by clicking your picture in the top right and then clicking Profile
foodsharingIdHelp.step4: On your profile you should see ID: XXXX
foodsharingIdHelp.imageAlt: Foodsharing profile page showing where to find the ID
foodsharingIdHelp.imageCaption: The number after ID is the one you need to enter.

admin.title: Admin dashboard
admin.onlyThreePickups: Only users with 3 or more pickups
adminAutomation.title: Slots Automation
adminAutomation.noAutomations: No automations match the current filter.
adminAutomation.modeActive: Automation is active and will run live.
adminAutomation.modeDryRun: Automation is in dry-run mode. Store-level dry-run cannot override this.
adminAutomation.modeDisabled: Automation is disabled at the application level.
adminAutomation.rulesIntro: Each store can enable its own rule set. During a run, the system evaluates every pending pickup request for enabled stores and records the result in the automation decisions table below.
adminAutomation.rulesRunTitle: What gets checked during a run
adminAutomation.rulesRunEnabledStores: Only stores with automation enabled are processed.
adminAutomation.rulesRunPendingOnly: Only pickup requests that are still waiting for confirmation are evaluated. Already confirmed requests are skipped.
adminAutomation.rulesRunOrder: Requests are checked store by store, and the decision reasons are saved for every evaluated request.
adminAutomation.rulesRunDryRunOverride: A store-level dry-run applies only to that store; the app-wide dry-run setting still forces dry-run globally.
adminAutomation.rulesRunDryRun: A dry-run uses the same logic but does not confirm or decline anything on Foodsharing. It only shows what would happen.
adminAutomation.rulesGracePeriodBody: If a pickup is less than 72 hours away, all automation rules are skipped and the request is allowed automatically.
adminAutomation.rulesGapSameStore: The gap rule checks whether the same person already has another pickup in the same store.
adminAutomation.rulesGapCalendarDays: The gap is calculated in calendar days in the Zurich timezone, excluding the pickup days themselves.
adminAutomation.rulesGapExample: Example: if the minimum gap is 2, a pickup on Monday and another on Wednesday is still too close because only Tuesday lies between them.
adminAutomation.rulesExperienceOwnHistor: A person counts as experienced in a store if Foodsharing membership data shows at least one previous pickup there.
adminAutomation.rulesExperienceCoPickup: If the person has no experience, the request can still pass when another person on the same pickup is experienced in that store.
adminAutomation.rulesCleaningExemption: If a Foodsharing ID has a cleaning exemption, the cleaning rule is skipped for that person.
adminAutomation.rulesCleaningHistory: Otherwise the system checks the configured cleaning store (`app.foodsharing.automation.cleaning-store-id`) within the configured lookback period (`app.foodsharing.automation.cleaning-back-check-months`).
adminAutomation.rulesCleaningFuture: The rule also passes if the person already has a future cleaning pickup planned.
adminAutomation.rulesCleaningOverride: If neither of those is true, the request is normally declined. It is only allowed as an override when fewer than four future cleaning slots are still empty.
adminAutomation.futurePickupUsers: Users with future pickups in your stores
